commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `codex` to both official Docker release `OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS` build-arg keep lists and adds a static regression assertion in `src/dockerfile.test.ts`.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main excludes `dist/extensions/codex/**`, the Docker prune script removes excluded plugin dist unless kept, and the release workflow keeps only `diagnostics-otel`. I did not run a full Docker build in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer real Docker image proof before merge; there is no narrow code repair for automation to attempt.

target.title: fix(docker): keep default OpenAI harness plugin in release images
target.body:
## Summary
- include the default OpenAI harness plugin in official Docker release keep lists
- add a workflow regression assertion so both release architectures keep that plugin

Closes #83613

## Real behavior proof
After the fix, the official Docker release workflow keeps the Codex plugin for both release architectures. Local terminal output from checking the workflow and Docker regression suite:

```text
$ rg "OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS=diagnostics-otel,codex" .github/workflows/docker-release.yml
158:            OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS=diagnostics-otel,codex
256:            OPENCLAW_EXTENSIONS=diagnostics-otel,codex

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/prune-docker-plugin-dist.test.ts src/dockerfile.test.ts test/scripts/docker-e2e-plan.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts
65 passed
```

## Tests
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/prune-docker-plugin-dist.test.ts src/dockerfile.test.ts test/scripts/docker-e2e-plan.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts
- git diff --check
target.author: yuanhanzhong
